title: Cardiac Phase Gating Paradigm
description: This protocol examines how cardiac phase (systole vs. diastole) modulates conscious perception thresholds. Stimuli are time-locked to the R-peak of the electrocardiogram (ECG) to deliver visual targets at defined cardiac phases. APGI predicts a significant cardiac-phase × ignition interaction mediated by interoceptive precision (Πⁱ_eff).

exclusion_criteria: Irregular ECG rhythm (assessed by study nurse)
Prior TMS contraindications (not relevant for this protocol; retained for series consistency)

apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG (Brain Products GmbH) at 5000 Hz
apparatus.display: VPixx ProPixx at 120 Hz, viewing distance 57 cm

dependent_variables: Estimated Sₜ at trial time (from fitted APGI model)
primary_hypothesis: Detection rate is higher at diastole than systole, consistent with elevated Πⁱ_eff during cardiac deceleration.
primary_analysis: Mixed-effects logistic regression: detection ~ cardiac_phase * contrast + (cardiac_phase | participant)
